Tomato___Early_blight: Apply copper-based fungicide or chlorothalonil according to label instructions.
Remove and destroy severely infected leaves to reduce inoculum.
Avoid overhead irrigation; water at the base of plants in the morning.
Rotate out of tomatoes and other solanaceous crops for at least 2–3 years.
Tomato___Late_blight: Immediately remove and destroy infected plants to limit spread.
Apply systemic fungicides recommended for late blight (consult local extension).
Avoid working in fields when foliage is wet.
Do not save seed or tubers from infected plants.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold: Improve air circulation by increasing plant spacing and pruning lower leaves.
Avoid overhead irrigation and high humidity in greenhouses.
Apply appropriate fungicides if disease pressure is high.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot: Use certified disease-free seed and transplants for future crops.
Remove and destroy infected plant material.
Avoid overhead irrigation and work in fields when foliage is dry.
Apply copper-based bactericides if recommended locally.
Tomato___healthy: Maintain balanced fertilization based on soil tests.
Water at the base of plants and avoid wetting foliage.
Scout regularly for early disease symptoms.
Rotate crops and remove crop residues after harvest.
Potato___Early_blight: Remove and destroy infected foliage and crop debris.
Apply recommended protectant fungicides at early signs of disease.
Avoid water stress and maintain balanced nitrogen levels.
Potato___Late_blight: Destroy infected plants promptly and avoid piling culls near fields.
Use protectant and systemic fungicides as per local guidelines.
Avoid overhead irrigation during cool, humid periods.
Potato___healthy: Use certified seed potatoes and rotate fields.
Hill soil around plants to protect developing tubers.
Monitor regularly for early symptoms of foliar diseases.
Apple___Apple_scab: Prune trees to improve air circulation and light penetration.
Remove and destroy fallen leaves and mummified fruit.
Apply fungicides at green tip through early fruit development if needed.
Apple___Black_rot: Remove and destroy cankered branches and mummified fruit.
Maintain good pruning practices to reduce humidity inside the canopy.
Apply fungicides during primary infection periods if recommended locally.
Apple___healthy: Prune annually to maintain an open canopy.
Sanitize orchard by removing fallen leaves and fruit.
Monitor for early disease symptoms and follow integrated pest management (IPM) guidelines.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_: Monitor fields early; fungicides are rarely needed except under severe pressure.
Avoid excessive nitrogen that may favor lush, susceptible foliage.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight: Use resistant hybrids suited to your region.
Rotate crops and manage corn residue to reduce inoculum.
Apply fungicides if recommended and economic thresholds are exceeded.
Corn_(maize)___healthy: Rotate with non-host crops to reduce disease pressure.
Plant certified seed and recommended hybrids.
Scout regularly and maintain balanced fertility.
Grape___Black_rot: Remove and destroy mummified fruit and infected shoots.
Prune to increase air movement and sunlight penetration.
Apply fungicides at key growth stages when conditions are wet and warm.
Grape___healthy: Train and prune vines properly to promote airflow.
Remove old clusters and debris from the vineyard floor.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening): Consult local agricultural extension immediately; this disease is serious and regulated.
Control the Asian citrus psyllid vector using approved insecticides and biological controls.
Remove and destroy severely affected trees if recommended by authorities.
default: Isolate and monitor the affected plant closely.
Remove heavily infected leaves or plant parts and dispose of them away from the field.
Avoid overhead irrigation; water at the base of the plant in the morning.
Ensure good air circulation and avoid overcrowding.
If disease persists or spreads, consult your local agricultural extension officer for region-specific fungicide or management recommendations.
